reasoning: Beta, I know Shantanu-da is a big minister now, and he is our Matua, yes — my husband even wanted me to vote for him. But I have seen so many ministers come and go, and I have heard promises about papers since before I was married. What I know for certain is the hand that gives me the rice card every month, and that hand is didi's hand. My son's fees, my daughter's bicycle — these things are real in my hand today. I cannot take the chance on words, even from a minister, when my family's belly is empty next month. Maybe one day Shantanu-da's papers will come, but until that day, didi's card is what keeps us alive.
primary_drivers: The monthly Khadya Sathi card that feeds six people — a tangible benefit from TMC that she depends on
Deep skepticism from lived experience — promises about CAA and citizenship documents have not materialized since 2019, 1991, 1972
Pragmatism about immediate survival over distant possibilities — even Shantanu's ministerial position feels abstract compared to the card in her pocket
